new_exact: respond to reasonable requests for information
evidence: PDF text shows '...by failing to respond to reasonable requests for information; and...' whereas DB omits 'requests for' and reads 'respond to reasonable information'. The omission causes the rule reference (1.4, Communication) to be incomplete—it should reference the duty to respond to 'requests for information' per N.D.R. Prof. Conduct 1.4.

PDF text shows 'Declining or Terminating Representation, by failing...' but DB shows 'Declining or Terminating requests Representation'. The word 'requests' appears to have migrated from the prior phrase ('respond to reasonable requests for information') and should not appear here.

source: PDF visual rendering (mutool draw -r 300) clearly shows 'submitted' in the rendered image; pdftotext extraction incorrectly converted it to 'sumbitted'
evidence: The rendered PDF page 1 at high resolution (300 DPI) displays the text 'Marina Spahr, Attorney General's Office, Bismarck, ND, for plaintiff and appellee; submitted on brief.' with correct spelling 'submitted'. The DB and pdftotext both show 'sumbitted', which is a text extraction artifact. The DB occurrence is byte-exact and occurs exactly once.

source: PDF/pdftotext comparison shows 'entry of judgment for' in authoritative PDF source (lines 238-242 of PDF), but DB text incorrectly omits 'judgment'
note: Located in paragraph [¶11] in the Rule 55(a)(1) text block. The word 'judgment' is essential to the rule text.

evidence: PDF line 18 shows lowercase 'v.' (the standard case separator abbreviation in legal captions). DB shows uppercase 'V.' Lowercase to uppercase confusion is a known OCR defect.
note: Legal convention requires lowercase 'v.' to separate parties in case names. Uppercase 'V.' violates standard caption formatting.

evidence: PDF shows 'affirm.' without Roman numeral I. The I is a section header that appears on its own line in the PDF (page 2) and should not be attached to the preceding sentence.
note: Roman numeral section header incorrectly merged with end of [¶1]

evidence: PDF shows citation ending with ').' (followed by page break and section II), not with ').' followed by Roman numeral III. The III appears as standalone section header on next page.
note: Roman numeral section header III incorrectly appended to citation at end of [¶5]

evidence: PDF shows period only: 'not all purposes.' The IV is a standalone section header on next page, not part of text.
note: Roman numeral section header IV incorrectly appended to end of [¶14]
